friction_items.description: 211 unique tool names registered in server.py (TOOL_DEFINITIONS dict). A qualitative interview scenario actively uses ~15-20 of them (sys_boot, tool_route, tool_plan_advance, sys_path_create, tool_python_exec, tool_qualitative_select_standard, tool_qualitative_codebook_diff, tool_qualitative_quote_provenance, tool_audit_step_completeness, tool_audit_synthesis, tool_audit_master, tool_synthesize, mem_log, sys_protocol_get, sys_protocol_log, tool_search, tool_path_finalize). Maintenance burden: every refactor touches a 7,500-line server.py; the _HANDLERS dict + TOOL_DEFINITIONS + per-tool handler are three places to update for one logical change. Discovery cost: even sys_semantic_tool_search requires the 'semantic' extra to be installed.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Phase 9 consolidation target: collapse the 6 audit_* tools into a single tool_audit(operation=...) dispatcher (kept as aliases). Same for the 4 tool_synthesize_* tools. Same for the 5 sys_checkpoint_*/sys_path_*/sys_config_* triads. Goal: 211 -> ~120 surface names, no behavioral change. Move per-handler code out of server.py into tools/actions/ submodules (already partially done) and shrink server.py to a dispatcher + TOOL_DEFINITIONS table only.

friction_items.description: FIVE qualitative protocols exist with overlapping triggers: (a) methodology/qualitative_research (core, in _router_index.yaml), (b) methodology/qualitative_pii_redaction (core), (c) methodology/qualitative_quality_audit (core), (d) qualitative pack: thematic_analysis_braun_clarke, coding_scheme_iteration, member_checking, qualitative_report_format. Maintainer pain: the pack's router_entries.py registers entries that are NOT in protocols/_router_index.yaml -- two sources of truth for routing. The core methodology/coding_scheme_development overlaps with the pack's coding_scheme_iteration. A prompt like 'code these 8 interviews' could route to either coding_scheme_development OR coding_scheme_iteration depending on which trigger wins. There is no documented precedence rule.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Pick ONE source of truth for router triggers (either _router_index.yaml OR pack router_entries.py, not both). Add a preflight check that fails if a protocol_id appears in both. Document the precedence in PROTOCOL_DOCTRINE.md. For the coding_scheme_development vs coding_scheme_iteration overlap, deprecate one (the pack version is more rigorous; deprecate the core one) with an alias for back-compat.

friction_items.description: PII redaction protocol (qualitative_pii_redaction) is policy-heavy (HIPAA Safe Harbor 18 classes, NER recall >= 0.95, false-negative gate) but there is NO maintainer-facing telemetry of how often the audit step BLOCKS in real usage, how often researchers bypass it, or what the median NER recall actually is in the field. mem_reliability_log captures gate_fire / override_used but the schema does not segment by protocol category, so 'qualitative gate-fire rate' cannot be answered without a one-off jq script. As maintainer I cannot prioritize hardening this protocol without that data.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Extend the reliability.jsonl schema with a 'protocol_category' field (qualitative / wet_lab / engineering / ...) populated automatically from the protocol id at log time. Add a tool_reliability_summary_by_category dispatcher (or a CLI flag) so a maintainer can run 'research-os doctor --reliability qualitative' and get a 30-day BLOCK / override / abandonment table. Pair with a docs/MAINTAINER_DASHBOARD.md describing how to read it.

friction_items.description: tool_qualitative_codebook_diff returns 'low_kappa_codes: [list]' with an advice string but does NOT return a structured failure code -- the caller (or the wrapping protocol step) has to string-match on the advice to know whether to BLOCK. tool_qualitative_select_standard returns 'Bundled checklist templates not found' when the bundled data dir is missing -- the error suggests 'reinstall research-os' but does not name WHICH path it tried (the loop checks 6 ancestors x 2 candidates = 12 paths). A maintainer debugging an install-broken report has to read _find_checklists_dir source to know what to look for.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Standardize tool errors on {status:'error', code:'<machine_readable>', message:'<human>', tried_paths:[...], suggested_actions:[...]} across the qualitative pack tools. For tool_qualitative_select_standard specifically, log the tried path list in the error message. For tool_qualitative_codebook_diff, add a 'gate_status' field ('pass' | 'block_low_kappa' | 'block_missing_inputs') so plan_advance can branch deterministically.

friction_items.description: Three places enforce participant anonymization: (1) methodology/qualitative_pii_redaction (ingest-time, HIPAA 18-class gate), (2) qualitative_quality_audit's quote-level anonymization check (synthesis-time), (3) the pack's member_checking step prepare_participant_materials manually says 'regex sweep + a re-read; there is no automated redactor in v1.9.3'. Three independent code paths for the same concept, and the third explicitly admits there is no tooling. As a maintainer I do not know which is canonical or how to evolve them coherently.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Extract de-identification into a single shared helper module (research_os.utils.pii) with one public function redact_text(text, policy, pseudonym_map). Have all three call sites consume it. Document the layering in docs/PROTOCOL_DOCTRINE.md: ingest = strong gate; synthesis = defence-in-depth audit; member-check materials = same helper, called with the participant-facing policy variant. Plan a deprecation of the ad-hoc regex sweep in member_checking.

friction_items.description: server.py is 7,499 lines (note: spec said baseline is 6,813; the file has grown 686 lines since baseline -- spec drift is itself a maintainer-facing signal). 211 tool definitions, 216 functions, all in one module. Adding the planned dedicated PII MCP wrapper (referenced as 'planned but not yet shipped' in the qualitative_pii_redaction protocol) will add yet more lines. Editing this file is slow (large diffs, slow grep, merge conflicts on every parallel feature branch).
friction_items.suggested_fix: Phase 9/10 deliverable: split server.py into (a) server.py = ~500 lines of MCP wiring + dispatcher only, (b) tools/registry.py = TOOL_DEFINITIONS table, (c) tools/handlers/<category>/*.py = handlers grouped by category (audit, synthesis, qualitative, routing, etc.). Pack tools already follow this pattern via register_tool decorator -- apply the same pattern to in-tree tools. Goal: no single file > 1500 lines.

friction_items.description: Several protocol descriptions assume deep methodological literacy without a glossary. thematic_analysis_braun_clarke.yaml editorial_voice references 'Lincoln & Guba 1985 trustworthiness criteria', 'codebook-TA', 'reflexive TA', 'semantic vs latent coding orientations', 'central organising concept' without defining any of them. coding_scheme_iteration references 'Strauss & Corbin', 'Landis & Koch 1977 table', 'theoretical saturation' similarly. A first-time maintainer reviewing a PR that touches these protocols has to do parallel literature reading before they can sanity-check the change.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add a docs/QUALITATIVE_GLOSSARY.md cross-referenced from each pack protocol's description (one-line link at the top). Cover the ~20 terms above. Cite the primary sources once in the glossary, not in every protocol. Reduces protocol description length AND lowers the maintainer onboarding cost.

friction_items.description: Pack protocols carry version 1.9.3 (e.g. member_checking, coding_scheme_iteration, thematic_analysis_braun_clarke) while core protocols carry 1.11.0 (qualitative_pii_redaction, qualitative_report_format). The release-prep doctrine in CLAUDE.md says 'bump every protocol YAML version field for MINOR/MAJOR' but pack protocols clearly did not get bumped on the 1.10 and 1.11 releases. Maintainer cannot tell from version alone whether a pack protocol is stale or merely unchanged.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Either (a) add a preflight check that the pack protocol versions are >= the core package version (and bump them on each release), OR (b) document explicitly in PROTOCOL_DOCTRINE.md that pack versions are independent and follow their own SemVer. Pick one stance and enforce it.

friction_items.description: tool_qualitative_codebook_diff iterates over (codes x segments) -- for an 8-participant study with ~150 codes and ~2000 segments that is 300k iterations, fine. But for 80 participants and 600 codes the inner loop becomes 6M iterations of dict lookups. No performance ceiling is documented and no benchmark exists in tests/. A maintainer cannot answer 'will this scale to 50 interviews?' without running it.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add a perf smoke test in tests/perf/ that runs codebook_diff on a synthetic 50-participant / 500-code corpus and asserts wall-time < 5s. Document the empirical scaling ceiling in the tool docstring. Same treatment for tool_qualitative_quote_provenance under high registry growth (jsonl append is O(1) but readers downstream may not be).

friction_items.description: PROTOCOL_DOCTRINE.md (per CLAUDE.md) says protocols are 'scaffolds for reasoning, not scripts to execute' and reviewers reject prescriptive protocols. But qualitative_pii_redaction.yaml is explicitly marked editorial_voice.mode: prescription and lists hard gates (BLOCK if policy missing a class, BLOCK on requires_review:true rows, BLOCK if false_negatives > 0). That is a defensible exception (compliance protocols ARE prescriptive) but the doctrine doesn't carve out the exception class. Future maintainers may either apply the doctrine too rigidly and weaken the gate, or use this protocol as license to make non-compliance protocols prescriptive.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add a 'Compliance protocols are an exception class' subsection to PROTOCOL_DOCTRINE.md, naming PII redaction, IRB review, and data ethics review as the canonical examples where mode: prescription is correct.

convoluted_tools: tool_audit_synthesis (master auditor that wraps 6 other audit tools and then itself is wrapped by tool_audit_master -- the layering is unclear)
tool_audit_master (claims to run all 6 quality gates but explicitly skips tool_audit_step_literature with a footnote -- inconsistent contract)
tool_plan_advance (returns status='blocked' for some gates but the override_gate parameter only applies to deliverable gates, not all gates -- caller cannot uniformly override)
tool_synthesize (12+ parameters including autopilot_full_assembly which silently errors in non-autopilot modes instead of being absent from the schema)
sys_boot (one-call bootstrap that replaces 4-5 separate calls -- great in principle but the response is now a deeply nested dict that callers have to navigate)

tool_route vs tool_semantic_route vs sys_semantic_tool_search (three overlapping routing surfaces; from a maintainer perspective, the precedence and fallback chain is documented inconsistently across their three short/description fields)
confusing_protocols: methodology/coding_scheme_development vs qualitative/coding/coding_scheme_iteration (overlapping triggers, two sources of truth, no precedence documented)

methodology/qualitative_research vs qualitative/method/thematic_analysis_braun_clarke (core protocol talks about COREQ/SRQR generally; pack protocol is method-specific; unclear when the router picks which)

methodology/qualitative_quality_audit vs qualitative/output/qualitative_report_format (both enforce COREQ/SRQR coverage -- one as a gate, one as a step; relationship between them not documented at the protocol level)

methodology/qualitative_pii_redaction (the doctrine says 'tool_python_exec script=scripts/qual_pii_redact.py' but that script is not shipped with research-os -- the protocol references a script the researcher has to write themselves, which is not surfaced clearly in the trigger or prerequisites)
never_called_tools: tool_dashboard_create (qualitative report goes through tool_synthesize, not dashboard)
tool_poster_create (qualitative interviews -> manuscript, not poster typically)
tool_latex_compile (qualitative reports usually go through pandoc/typst, not latex)
tool_adapter_extract, tool_adapters_list, tool_adapters_run_all (no slurm/synapse/cytoscape/nextflow/redcap/snakemake in a qualitative interview workflow)
tool_audit_figure_full, tool_figure_palette (no figures in this scenario beyond the optional thematic_map_rev*.png and saturation_curve.png, which the researcher draws by hand)
tool_preregister_diff (qualitative reflexive TA is not preregistered the way RCTs are; this gate adds noise)
tool_workflow_dag, tool_step_env_lock (compute-heavy reproducibility tools that don't apply to a transcript-coding workflow)
tool_env_snapshot, tool_env_docker_generate (same -- no compute env to capture)
tool_paper_figures_autoembed (figures are optional; this gate fires false-positively)
sys_export_share_archive (potentially relevant for IRB compliance but never invoked in the happy path)
recommendations: Phase 9 consolidation: target 211 -> ~120 tool surface names via operation-dispatcher consolidation of the audit_*, synthesize_*, checkpoint_*, path_*, config_* groups (with back-compat aliases). Track in CHANGELOG as a MAJOR with a Migration section.

Phase 10 modularization: split server.py (7,499 lines) into server.py (dispatcher + MCP wiring, ~500 lines) + tools/registry.py + tools/handlers/<category>/*.py. Apply the same register_tool decorator pattern packs already use.
Single source of truth for routing: pick either _router_index.yaml OR pack router_entries.py and enforce it via preflight. Document precedence in PROTOCOL_DOCTRINE.md.

Resolve the qualitative protocol overlap: deprecate methodology/coding_scheme_development in favor of the pack's coding_scheme_iteration (with alias) and document the layering between methodology/qualitative_research and the pack's method-specific protocols.
Extract de-identification into research_os.utils.pii with one canonical helper; refactor qualitative_pii_redaction, qualitative_quality_audit, and member_checking to consume it.

Ship the planned first-class MCP wrapper for PII redaction so the qualitative_pii_redaction protocol stops pointing at a researcher-authored scripts/qual_pii_redact.py that does not exist in the package.
Add protocol_category segmentation to reliability.jsonl and a tool_reliability_summary_by_category dispatcher so maintainers can answer 'qualitative gate-fire rate over 30 days' without ad-hoc jq.
Standardize tool error envelope: {status, code, message, tried_paths?, suggested_actions[]}. Refactor qualitative pack tools as the pilot.
Add docs/QUALITATIVE_GLOSSARY.md and replace inline literature dumps in pack protocol descriptions with a single link reference.
Either bump pack protocol versions on every release OR document independent SemVer in PROTOCOL_DOCTRINE.md -- stop the drift.
Add tests/perf/ smoke test for tool_qualitative_codebook_diff scaling to 50+ participants; document the empirical ceiling in the docstring.
Add a 'Compliance protocols are an exception class' subsection to PROTOCOL_DOCTRINE.md so the prescriptive mode of qualitative_pii_redaction stops looking like a doctrine violation.
